edges.description: Loss of the COX4-1 subunit yields a catalytically deficient holoenzyme.
edges.source: COX4-1 Subunit Loss and Failed Complex IV Assembly
edges.target: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: A dysfunctional Complex IV leaks reactive oxygen species, driving oxidative stress and genomic instability.
edges.source: COX4-1 Subunit Loss and Failed Complex IV Assembly

edges.description: Loss of oxidative ATP synthesis forces anaerobic glycolysis and lactate accumulation.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: The bioenergetic deficit is most damaging in high oxidative-demand tissues, notably the brain.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Oxidative stress is associated with increased chromosomal breaks that simulate Fanconi anemia.
Lactate overproduction manifests clinically as lactic acidosis.
Bioenergetic failure in the brain produces encephalopathy and developmental regression.

nodes.description: COX4I1 encodes the common (ubiquitous) isoform of cytochrome c oxidase subunit 4, a nuclear-encoded structural and regulatory subunit of Complex IV. Biallelic COX4I1 variants reduce or abolish COX4-1 expression; in the index case the COX4I1 mRNA was decreased and the COX4 protein undetectable. Loss of this core subunit destabilizes the holoenzyme and yields an isolated Complex IV biogenesis defect.
nodes.id: COX4-1 Subunit Loss and Failed Complex IV Assembly

biological_processes: mitochondrial respiratory chain complex IV assembly

evidence.reference_title: Mutation in the COX4I1 gene is associated with short stature, poor weight gain and increased chromosomal breaks, simulating Fanconi anemia.
evidence.snippet: This nuclear gene encodes the common isoform of cytochrome c oxidase (COX) subunit 4 (COX 4-1), an integral regulatory part of COX (respiratory chain complex IV) the terminal electron acceptor of the mitochondrial respiratory chain.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation in the COX4I1 gene is associated with short stature, poor weight gain and increased chromosomal breaks, simulating Fanconi anemia.
evidence.snippet: decreased expression of COX4I1 mRNA and undetectable (COX4) protein

nodes.description: Encephalopathy, a core feature of the severe (P152T) sibling presentation.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic variants in COX4I1 associated with a novel phenotype resembling Leigh syndrome with developmental regression, intellectual disability, and seizures.
evidence.snippet: two siblings, born to consanguineous parents, who presented with encephalopathy, developmental regression, hypotonia

nodes.description: Energy failure is most damaging in high oxidative-demand tissues. In COX4I1-related disease the dominant tropism in the severe presentation is the central nervous system, producing encephalopathy, developmental regression, and Leigh-syndrome-like brain imaging; growth failure reflects systemic energy deficit.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic variants in COX4I1 associated with a novel phenotype resembling Leigh syndrome with developmental regression, intellectual disability, and seizures.
evidence.snippet: we describe two siblings, born to consanguineous parents, who presented with encephalopathy, developmental regression, hypotonia, pathognomonic brain imaging findings resembling Leigh-syndrome, and a novel homozygous variant on COX4I1

nodes.description: Deficient Complex IV blocks transfer of electrons from cytochrome c to molecular oxygen and the coupled proton pumping, reducing oxidative ATP synthesis. Patient fibroblasts showed decreased COX activity and impaired ATP production, both rescued by re-expression of wild-type COX4I1.
nodes.id: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

biological_processes: mitochondrial electron transport, cytochrome c to oxygen

evidence.reference_title: Mutation in the COX4I1 gene is associated with short stature, poor weight gain and increased chromosomal breaks, simulating Fanconi anemia.
evidence.snippet: The patient's fibroblasts disclosed decreased COX activity, impaired ATP production, elevated ROS production

evidence.reference_title: Mutation in the COX4I1 gene is associated with short stature, poor weight gain and increased chromosomal breaks, simulating Fanconi anemia.
evidence.snippet: COX activity and ATP production were restored by lentiviral transfection with the wild-type gene.

nodes.description: Increased chromosomal breaks simulating Fanconi anemia, the distinctive finding that delayed the genetic diagnosis in the index case.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation in the COX4I1 gene is associated with short stature, poor weight gain and increased chromosomal breaks, simulating Fanconi anemia.
evidence.snippet: Mutation in the COX4I1 gene is associated with short stature, poor weight gain and increased chromosomal breaks, simulating Fanconi anemia.

nodes.description: With oxidative phosphorylation impaired, pyruvate is shunted to lactate, elevating blood and CSF lactate; metabolic stress can precipitate decompensation in the encephalopathic (Leigh-like) presentation.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic variants in COX4I1 associated with a novel phenotype resembling Leigh syndrome with developmental regression, intellectual disability, and seizures.
evidence.snippet: Defective production of cytochrome c oxidase leads to a variable phenotypic spectrum ranging from isolated myopathy to Leigh syndrome.

nodes.description: Elevated blood and CSF lactate from the bioenergetic block in cytochrome c oxidase, the clinical manifestation of the "Lactic Acidosis and Metabolic Decompensation" pathophysiology node and a defining biochemical feature of the Leigh-syndrome-spectrum presentation of COX4I1 deficiency.

A distinctive feature of COX4-1 deficiency is elevated reactive oxygen species production accompanying the respiratory-chain defect. In the index case this was associated with increased chromosomal breakage that clinically simulated Fanconi anemia, illustrating how a primary Complex IV subunit defect can mimic a nuclear DNA-repair disorder.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation in the COX4I1 gene is associated with short stature, poor weight gain and increased chromosomal breaks, simulating Fanconi anemia.
evidence.snippet: The patient's fibroblasts disclosed decreased COX activity, impaired ATP production, elevated ROS production

evidence.reference_title: Mutation in the COX4I1 gene is associated with short stature, poor weight gain and increased chromosomal breaks, simulating Fanconi anemia.
evidence.snippet: We describe a novel autosomal recessive form of mitochondrial disease in a child with short stature, poor weight gain, and mild dysmorphic features with highly suspected Fanconi anemia due to a mutation in COX4I1 gene.
